question: Does aggressive daily submission usage improve calibration enough to justify using 4-5 slots?
why_now: Public score is the only true feedback signal, but submissions are capped.
candidate_options: Conservative 1-2 submissions per day | aggressive 4-5 designed submissions per day | mixed schedule near deadlines
selection_reason: More public-score observations improve low-dimensional calibration if candidates are audited and non-duplicate.
evidence_needed: daily submission ledger plus public scores
next_question: Define the first daily batch template.

question: What should drive each batch of work?
why_now: Avoid disconnected parameter tweaks and public leaderboard overfit.
candidate_options: Ad hoc candidate edits | question-driven batch loop | fixed model-only roadmap
selection_reason: Questions force each submission to answer a reusable uncertainty and produce the next decision.
evidence_needed: goal file review and future batch records
next_question: Create first concrete candidate question backlog.
